topic: Meta launched AI auto creative where you give it a URL and a budget and it handles creative targetin
content:
Meta's new AI ad tool isn't replacing marketers. It's exposing weak offers.

When the AI handles targeting, bidding, and creative, the offer is all that's left.

I ran 3 campaigns last week. Same budget. Same product category. Same 7-day window.

Campaign 1. Sharp offer, 1.8 second landing page. CPA dropped 34% vs the manual build.

Campaign 2. Generic offer, 4 second page.
CPA went up 22%. The AI tried 47 creative variants.
None of them saved it.

Campaign 3. Homepage with 5 CTAs.
The algorithm couldn't pick a goal.
Spend burned, conversions flat.

Auto creative scales whatever you give it.
A strong offer scales fast.
Weak ones scale the loss faster.

What it does well:
- Generates 30 to 50 creative variants from one URL
- Picks targeting from full funnel signal, not just the click
- Bids in real time across placements

What it can't do:
- Fix a page converting at 0.5%
- Write an offer worth clicking on
- Build the trust that makes someone choose you over the next ad

I put the full breakdown in the Meta Auto Creative Playbook.
7 things to prep before you hand over the budget.

1. Connect with me.
2. Comment PLAYBOOK and I'll send it over.

Curious if anyone else has tested this yet.
What did your CPA do in the first week?
